methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Karman Industries is a Seed-stage company in Climate & Energy founded by David Tearse and Chiranjeev Kalra, having raised $11.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) operating in the same sector, including Series A-stage companies such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two triangulation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-Progression uses the $11.5M capital raised as a proxy baseline, applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x, yielding an implied valuation of $29M. Peer-Median methodology examines the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which traded at a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M. The disparity between methods reflects the gap between Seed-stage companies and Series A peers.

Triangulating across both methods produces a valuation range of $24M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty regarding Karman's relative positioning within its cohort. Key drivers of value include the strength of the founding team and technical differentiation relative to the peer set. Resolution of these uncertainties through customer validation or technical milestones would be expected to narrow the range in subsequent financing rounds.
